title: As Trump Cools on NATO, Turkey Hosts Summit Meeting
summary: As President Trump cools on the alliance, its members see Turkey’s large military and vibrant defense sector as assets.

title: Deadly Russian Strikes Rock Kyiv on Eve of NATO Summit
summary: Ballistic missiles were fired into the capital of Ukraine as Russia launched its second major attack in less than a week. At least 12 people were killed, officials said.

title: Ukraine Remade Air Defense, but Russia Has Changed Its Attacks
summary: Ukraine has changed how militaries use Patriot systems. But recent attacks prove that clever adaptations cannot overcome a shortage of interceptors.

title: Erdogan-Trump Friendship Can Help NATO, Turkey’s Foreign Minister Says
summary: In an interview, Turkey’s top diplomat, Hakan Fidan, said the relationship between President Recep Tayyip Erdogan and President Trump could ease NATO tensions.

title: Huge Crowds Gather for Ayatollah’s Funeral, and China Tests Long-Range Ballistic Missile
summary: Plus, an unusual World Cup reversal.

title: Belgium Expresses Shock After FIFA Lifts U.S. Player’s Suspension
summary: The American striker Folarin Balogun is eligible to play in a World Cup match against Belgium on Monday night after soccer officials reversed his one-game ban.

title: In Trump’s Second Midterm, Democrats Are Looking for Fighters
summary: Attitudes in the party have shifted, Democrats say, with a growing appetite for outsider candidates vowing to overhaul the system. Some worry that the trend could be costly in November.

title: Mallory McMorrow Suspends Senate Bid in Democrats’ Heated Michigan Primary
summary: The state senator sought to carve a middle path between a left-wing candidate and one backed by the party establishment, but she gained little traction.

title: China Test Fires Long-Range Ballistic Missile in the Pacific
summary: China’s firing of a submarine-launched missile came as Australia secured more defense deals with Pacific Island nations. Countries expressed concern about the test.

title: Fatigue, Tension, Joy: England Pulls a World Cup All-Nighter
summary: The game against Mexico started at 2 a.m. in Britain. Pubs were allowed to stay open unusually late for fans to share the drama together.

title: The Funeral of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
summary: Iran’s new leaders are commemorating Ayatollah Khamenei, who was killed on the first day of the U.S.-Israeli war against the country, with a funeral unlike any other in the country’s recent history.

title: Emil Bove Defended Trump in Court. Then Trump Made Him a Judge.
summary: President Trump is appointing his former personal lawyers to judgeships, part of a larger effort to put his stamp on the federal courts.

title: How Trump Put Himself in the Middle of America’s 250th
summary: The president placed his image and initiatives front and center, blurring the line, critics said, between loyalty to nation and loyalty to him.

title: For Families With Nonspeaking Autistic Children, a Fierce Debate Over Assisted Spelling
summary: Popular communication methods for nonspeaking autistic people have ignited a fierce debate over what counts as evidence of hidden cognitive abilities.

title: With $8 Billion in Venezuelan Oil Money, U.S. Gives $300 Million in Quake Aid
summary: In 2010, there was a huge U.S. aid effort after a quake in Haiti. Today, U.S. officials say they oversee billions in Venezuelan oil revenue, but aid is less.

title: Sites That Report Venezuela’s Missing Rise Far From Quake Zone
summary: Despite problems like duplicate submissions, tallies gathered by the websites have been cited by news organizations and critics of the Venezuelan government’s disaster response.

title: Research Universities Are Admitting Fewer Ph.D.s, a Bad Sign for Science
summary: Some of the nation’s top research universities are shrinking doctoral programs because of uncertain federal funding.

title: Bring Back the SAT, California
summary: Under the test-blind policy, more students are thrown into classes for which they are unprepared.

title: The Liberal American Jew’s Dilemma
summary: Over the years, I have moved toward the way of being Jewish that my father rejected.

title: The Latest News
summary: Catch up after the long weekend.

title: Andy Burnham, the UK’s Likely Next Prime Minister, Made His Name in Manchester. Here’s How.
summary: Andy Burnham was mayor of Greater Manchester for nine years. His time there came to define his identity — and his pitch to lead Britain.
